error_signature: %error: <file>:<n>: number is missing value digits: <n>'d <n> | <n>: get_coeff = <n>'d -<n>; | ^~~~~ %error: <file>:<n>: number is missing value digits: <n>'d <n> | <n>: get_coeff = <n>'d -<n>; | ^~~~~ %error: exiting due to <n> error(s)
raw_error_example:
%Error: output/direct_16.sv:25:32: Number is missing value digits: 10'd 
   25 |                 0: get_coeff = 10'd -1;
      |                                ^~~~~
%Error: output/direct_16.sv:41:33: Number is missing value digits: 10'd 
   41 |                 16: get_coeff = 10'd -1;
      |                                 ^~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
root_cause: The compiler cannot parse the negative number literal because the minus sign is placed after the base specifier (e.g., `10'd -1`), which is invalid syntax.
fix_rule: Move the minus sign before the width and base specifier to write a negative constant (e.g., `-10'd1`). For a zero coefficient, use only the positive form (e.g., `10'd0`).
